raw_output:

The Good Friday Agreement, also known as the Belfast Agreement, was a landmark agreement signed on April 10, 1998, in Belfast, Northern Ireland. It aimed to bring an end to the decades-long conflict in Northern Ireland by establishing a power-sharing government, dissolving paramilitary groups, and promoting a peaceful resolution. The agreement paved the way for a new era of stability in the region and helped to unite the island of Ireland under a single government.
